steps.title: Identify the goal and the key note
steps.content: The student wants to define the term "high-sinuosity river." The relevant note states: "A river with high sinuosity has many meanders, and a river with low sinuosity has few." Combined with the definition of meanders ("U-shaped curve in a river channel"), that note contains everything needed for a clean definition.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Over time, the many meanders in the high-sinuosity Nestucca River will change shape and migrate downstream.’: This describes what happens to a specific example river over time — it doesn't define the term. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘A high-sinuosity river is one that has many meanders, or U-shaped curves.’: This directly defines the term using the notes' own language: many meanders, clarified as U-shaped curves. No extra behavioral details or specific examples. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘High sinuosity is caused by the erosion and redepositing of sediment in a riverbank over time.’: This describes a cause of meanders, not a definition of high sinuosity. The notes say erosion creates meanders, not that it defines the sinuosity level. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘A high-sinuosity river has U-shaped curves called meanders that will gradually change shape and shift downstream.’: This mixes the definition with behavioral details about how meanders change over time, which are irrelevant to defining the term. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
